title: hieracium andrasovszkyi subsp. cremnophilum
intro: Hieracium andrasovszkyi subsp. cremnophilum, also known as Andrasovszky's Hawkweed, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the mountainous regions of Europe. It belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Hieracium andrasovszkyi subsp. cremnophilum is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
